data.title: 2025 Gun Violence stats show progress, urge for prevention. Omnilert report highlights positive shifts in indicators.
data.description: Omnilert, based in Leesburg, Virginia, a prominent provider of AI-driven safety and emergency communication solutions, has recently published its 2025 report on Gun Violence. The report reveals positive changes in significant metrics, stressing the importance of multi-layered security measures and swift reaction to emergencies. This information was announced on January 14, 2026.

data.title: Gantry's 2025 commercial mortgage production hits $4.2 billion, 25% up YoY with $1.5 billion in Q4 alone.
data.description: In San Francisco, Gantry announced a significant increase in its commercial mortgage production for 2025, totaling $4.2 billion. This marks a notable 25% year-over-year growth, with the fourth quarter alone contributing $1.5 billion to the total production.

data.title: Eagle Pharma sells Barhemsys (amisulpride) rights in U.S. to LXO Group, Paris, under OTCMKTS: EGRX, effective Jan. 14, 2026.
data.description: Eagle Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (OTCMKTS: EGRX) based in Woodcliff Lake, N.J., disclosed today its decision to divest the marketing authorization for Barhemsys (amisulpride) in the U.S. The Company will transfer this authorization to LXO Group, headquartered in Paris, France. This strategic move signifies Eagle's focus on its core operations and expansion plans. The specifics of the deal, including financial terms and duration, were not publicly disclosed. The transaction is part of Eagle's larger business strategy and marks a significant development for both companies involved.

data.title: Quantix partners with MC Tank Lines & Leahy Transport to enhance liquid transport network, boosting capabilities.
data.description: On January 14, 2026, in Houston, Quantix, a prominent supply chain services provider for the chemical sector in North America, disclosed its latest strategic collaborations with MC Tank Lines and Leahy Transport. These partnerships aim to enhance Quantix's transportation network and broaden its range of services. The move signifies a bolstering of capabilities and a significant expansion in reach within the industry.

data.title: Centria Autism addresses BCBA shortage through Elevate program and UNR partnership. - 84 characters
data.description: The Elevate practicum program, along with a collaboration with the University of Nevada, Reno, has been making significant strides in transforming the industry. Centria Autism, a leading provider of therapeutic services for children with autism nationwide, based in Farmington Hills, Michigan, announced the progress after six months on Jan. 14, 2026. This initiative has had a noticeable impact, reflecting the growing importance of partnerships and training programs.

data.title: Amplifire's new training approach results in 75% reduction in training time, higher provider satisfaction.
data.description: In a recent report by the KLAS Arch Collaborative, it was highlighted that an Adaptive Learning Platform based in Boulder, Colorado, has successfully provided personalized Electronic Health Record (EHR) training on a large scale. This initiative has led to heightened provider satisfaction levels and remarkable efficiency improvements. According to the report released on January 14, 2026, Amplifire, recognized for its expertise in adaptive learning and employee development, has significantly impacted EHR training outcomes. The platform's approach has resulted in higher satisfaction rates among providers, demonstrating a notable increase in efficiency gains within the healthcare sector.

data.title: Primech AI's Hytron showcased in Havas CES 2026 Super Stream, boosting visibility to global enterprise audience.
data.description: Hytron, the latest innovation from Primech AI, was featured in a floor tour at Havas Super Stream CES 2026. This event provided a platform for the product to gain exposure to enterprise facility operators and brand leaders, widening its reach within the industry. This opportunity allowed Hytron to showcase its capabilities and benefits to a significant audience.

data.title: PPR Capital Management raised $142 million in 2025, surpassing $1 billion total raised and reinvested; attracting hundreds of new investors.
data.description: A company based in Wayne, Pennsylvania, PPR Capital Management, has exceeded $1 billion in funds raised and reinvested as part of its largest yearly expansion yet, reflecting robust investor confidence. Ending 2025 on a high note, the firm secured $142 million in new capital while also seeing a growth in their investor base by hundreds.

data.title: Squabble introduced Sophie, AI to improve US justice system for millions. Founder aims to simplify legal process.
data.description: A legal tech company in Los Angeles, Squabble, has introduced an enhanced AI system to simplify the process of filing claims in small claims court. Michael Egenthal, the founder of Squabble, highlighted that many individuals feel overwhelmed and uncertain about the legal process. Despite small-claims court being known as the 'people’s court', it is often perceived as complex and intimidating. By leveraging AI technology, Squabble aims to address these challenges and make the process more accessible to the public. This development is poised to provide a more user-friendly experience for individuals navigating the legal system, marking a significant advancement in promoting justice.

data.title: Middle East B2C Ecommerce market set to grow by 10% annually, reaching $146.9 billion by 2025.
data.description: A new report on the Middle East B2C Ecommerce Market reveals promising projections. The market in the Middle East and Africa (MEA) is forecasted to experience significant growth, with an expected annual expansion rate of 10%. By 2025, the market is anticipated to reach a size of around US$146.9 billion. The region demonstrated noteworthy progress from 2020 to 2024, showing a compound annual growth rate. This data has been compiled in the "Middle East B2C Ecommerce Market Size & Forecast by Value and Volume Across 80+ KPIs - Databook Q4 2025 Update" report, recently made available through ResearchAndMarkets.com.

data.title: Skild AI secures $1.4B investment led by SoftBank, with Jeff Bezos and others participating, now valued at over $14B.
data.description: Skild AI, a company specializing in AI robotics and the development of a scalable foundation model for robotics, has recently secured funding totaling nearly $1.4 billion. The funding round was led by SoftBank Group, with NVentures (NVIDIA’s venture capital branch), Macquarie Capital, Jeff Bezos (via Bezos Expeditions), Disruptive, and 1789 Capital also participating. Existing investors such as Lightspeed, Felicis, Coatue, and Sequoia Capital have increased their investment in the company. Additionally, several strategic investors have joined the funding initiative.
